Leading the Way
in Mobility
                                       We are Hero MotoCorp, the world’s largest                                                                                   Vision
                                       manufacturer of motorcycles and scooters                                                                BE THE FUTURE OF MOBILITY
                                       for 24 consecutive years. We have been
                                       a transformative force in the global two-
                                       wheeler industry, enabling personal mobility
                                       at scale while redefining value, trust, and
                                       innovation.
                                                                                                                                                                  Mission
                                       Since our inception in 1984, we have sold over 124 million two-
                                       wheelers across more than 48 countries, driving aspirations and                                     CREATE I COLLABORATE I INSPIRE
                                       progress worldwide. Guided by our vision, we combine advanced
                                       technology, customer insights, and strategic foresight to deliver
                                       accessible, sustainable, and future-ready mobility solutions.

                                       FY 2024-25 was a defining year for us. We expanded our global
                                       footprint by entering new markets such as the Philippines, launched
                                       segment-leading products, and accelerated innovation across our
                                       product range, digital platforms, and customer experience.
                                                                                                                                                                   Values
                                       From pioneering mobility in India to advancing international
                                       motorsports, we remain committed to shaping the future of mobility                                   PASSION I INTEGRITY I RESPECT I
                                       responsibly, inclusively, and at scale.                                                              COURAGE I BEING RESPONSIBLE

                                                                   underscored our ability to respond

                                                                                   Nacho Cornejo Joins Hero MotoSports
                                                                                   In April 2024, Hero MotoSports strengthened its roster by onboarding Chilean
                                                                                   rally ace José Ignacio 'Nacho' Cornejo. With nine Dakar appearances, which
                                                                                   includes six Top-10 finishes, and nine stage wins, the winner of FIM Cross
                                                                                   Country Rallies Junior Championship 2016 brings proven pedigree. After six
                                                                                   successful years with Honda Racing, Nacho’s entry marks a bold step in Hero’s
                                                                                   quest for global rally dominance.

                                                                                                                       Beyond EVs, we are building low-carbon, future-compliant engine platforms. Our models — from Splendor FE to
                                                                                                                       Xpulse 210 — now integrate lightweight designs, ethanol compatibility and BS6/OBD-2B compliance.

Riding the Wave of                                                                                                                                    Rise of Electric Two-Wheelers                                                                           Our Response

Economic Momentum                                                                                                                                     Indian consumers are increasingly
                                                                                                                                                      embracing electric two-wheelers, driven by
                                                                                                                                                      lower total cost of ownership and a rising
                                                                                                                                                      awareness of environmental sustainability.
                                                                                                                                                      This shift is gaining momentum with
                                                                                                                                                                                                       solidifying electric two-wheelers as a
                                                                                                                                                                                                       vital pillar in India’s mobility future reach
                                                                                                                                                                                                       well beyond metros into rural and semi-
                                                                                                                                                                                                       urban markets.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          We began our electric mobility journey
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          with the launch of the VIDA range
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          in FY 2022-23. In FY 2024-25, we
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          updated the range with the launch
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          of V2 range, offering three variants.
                                                                                                                                                      strong policy support, most recent is the                                                           We are consistently strengthening the

                                                                                                                                                      lithium-ion batteries. The continued                                                                enhancing customer experience. Vida is
is set for robust expansion. This growth is driven by increasing per capita income,                                                                   advancement in lithium-ion battery                                                                  now available across 360+ cities with a
the revival of rural demand, and improved credit accessibility.                                                                                       performance, coupled with declining costs,
                                                                                                                                                      is enhancing the appeal of EVs. Additionally,

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          280+ cities. Strategic partnerships

Leading with                                                           Governance Framework                         Code of Conduct for the                       Ethics


Ethics and Accountability
                                                                       At the heart of our corporate governance     Board and Employees                           We uphold uncompromising ethical
                                                                       approach lies a strong, well-structured      Our comprehensive Code of Conduct             standards across all our global
                                                                       framework led by a diverse and               governs the behaviour of the Board,           operations. Regardless of regional

design, implementation and maintenance of adequate internal         reference to Standalone Financial Statements

the orderly and efficient conduct of its business, including        standalone financial statements is a process designed to
adherence to the company’s policies, the safeguarding of its        provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of
assets, the prevention and detection of frauds and errors, the      financial reporting and the preparation of financial statements
accuracy and completeness of the accounting records, and the        for external purposes in accordance with generally accepted
timely preparation of reliable financial information, as required   accounting principles. A company’s internal financial
under the Companies Act, 2013.                                      control with reference to standalone financial statements

(c) The income tax expense for the year can be reconciled to the accounting profit as follows:                                                     35. Segment Information
                                                                                                      For the year ended    For the year ended     	
                                                                                                                                                    The Company primarily operates in the automotive segment. The automotive segment includes all activities related to
      Particulars
                                                                                                         March 31, 2025        March 31, 2024       development, design, manufacture, assembly and sale of vehicles, as well as sale of related parts and accessories. The board of
      Profit before tax                                                                                       6,127.81              5,258.36        directors of the Company, who has been identified as being the chief operating decision maker (CODM), evaluates the Company's

      Two wheelers                                                                                                         478.09               744.58            Investment Risk           The present value of the defined benefit plan liability (denominated in Indian Rupee) is calculated using a discount

  plan provides for a lump sum payment to the employees at the time of separation from the service on completion of vested
  year of employment. The liability of gratuity plan is provided based on actuarial valuation as at the end of each financial year

  the plan assets are maintained and for certain group companies the liability of gratuity plan is provided based on actuarial
  valuation as at the end of each financial year.

37. Segment Information
                                                                                                                                                       Niloy Motors Limited; Bangladesh
	The Group primarily operates in the automotive segment. The automotive segment includes all activities related to
  development, design, manufacture, assembly and sale of vehicles, as well as sale of related parts and accessories. The                               Nitol Motors Limited; Bangladesh
  board of directors of the Holding Company, which has been identified as being the chief operating decision maker (CODM),
                                                                                                                                                       Nitol Machineries Limited; Bangladesh
  evaluates the Group's performance, allocate resources based on the analysis of the various performance indicator of the
  Group as a single unit.                                                                                                                              Nitol Insurance Company Limited; Bangladesh
	Therefore, based on the guiding principles given in Ind AS 108 on ‘Operating Segments’, the Group’s business activity fall
  within a single operating segment, namely automotive segment.

  Group. The Group has adopted a policy of only dealing with creditworthy counterparties as a means of mitigating the risk
